---
title: "Exploratory Data Analysis"
format: 
  dashboard:
    scrolling: true
server: shiny
---

```{r setup}
#| context: setup
library(shiny)
library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(scales)
library(tidyverse)
library(here)
library(gt)

# Load and prep data
all_data <- read_csv(here("data", "clean_data.csv"))

response_base_data <- all_data %>%
  mutate(
    subj_response_clean = factor(
      subj_response,
      levels = c("allgreen", "mostlygreen", "evensplit", "mostlyred", "allred"),
      labels = c("All\nGreen", "Mostly\nGreen", "Mixed", "Mostly\nRed", "All\nRed")
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(subj_response_clean))

all_participants <- sort(unique(as.character(response_base_data$participant)))
```

# Subjective Responses

## Row

### Sidebar {.sidebar}

```{r menu}
shiny::selectInput(
  inputId = "subject_select",
  label = "Select Subjects to Compare:",
  # FIXED: Added the aggregated label string option directly into the choices pool
  choices = c("Across Subjects (Aggregated)", all_participants),
  selected = "Across Subjects (Aggregated)",
  multiple = TRUE
)

shiny::actionButton("select_all", "Select All Subjects", class = "btn-block")
shiny::actionButton("clear_all", "Clear All Selections", class = "btn-block")
shiny::actionButton("across_subjects", "Across Subjects", class = "btn-block")
```

### Column: Interactive Participant Grid

```{r colorplot}
plotlyOutput("color_plot", height = "500px")
```

```{r choose server}
#| context: server

# Update when selection is made
observeEvent(input$select_all, {
  updateSelectInput(session, "subject_select", selected = all_participants)
})

observeEvent(input$clear_all, {
  updateSelectInput(session, "subject_select", selected = character(0))
})

observeEvent(input$across_subjects, {
  updateSelectInput(session, "subject_select", selected = "Across Subjects (Aggregated)")
})

# wrap everything in renderPlotly
output$color_plot <- renderPlotly({
  req(input$subject_select)
  
  if ("Across Subjects (Aggregated)" %in% input$subject_select) {
    
    # Calculate for across subject 
    plot_data <- response_base_data %>%
      count(subj_response_clean) %>%
      mutate(prop = n / sum(n))
    
    # Across subject plot (no faceting)
    p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = subj_response_clean, y = prop, fill = subj_response_clean)) +
      geom_col(width = 0.65)
    
  } else {
    
# Calculations for each subject selected
    plot_data <- response_base_data %>%
      filter(participant %in% input$subject_select) %>%
      count(participant, subj_response_clean) %>%
      group_by(participant) %>%
      mutate(prop = n / sum(n)) %>%
      ungroup()
    
# plot with facet option for displaying multiple subjects
    p <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = subj_response_clean, y = prop, fill = subj_response_clean)) +
      geom_col(width = 0.65) +
      facet_wrap(~ participant, ncol = 3)
  }
  
  # graph customization
  p <- p +
    scale_x_discrete(expand = expansion(mult = 0.15)) +
    scale_fill_manual(values = c(
      "All\nGreen" = "#1b7837", "Mostly\nGreen" = "#7fbf7b",
      "Mixed" = "#d9d9d9", "Mostly\nRed" = "#ef8a62", "All\nRed" = "#b2182b"
    )) +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = percent_format(), limits = c(0, 1)) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
    theme(
      legend.position = "none", 
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 30, hjust = 1),
      strip.text = element_text(face = "bold")
    )
  
  ggplotly(p)
})
```

# Probe Accuracy

## Row

```{r accuracy plot}
##prepare data
accuracy_data <- all_data %>%
  mutate(
    trial_type_clean = case_when(
      trial_type == "control" ~ "Control",
      trial_type %in% c("congruent") ~ "Probe = Imagined Color",
      trial_type == "incongruent" ~ "Probe \u2260 Imagined Color",
      TRUE ~ NA_character_
    )
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(trial_type_clean)) %>%
  group_by(participant, trial_type_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    prop_correct = mean(correct, na.rm = TRUE),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    trial_type_clean = factor(
      trial_type_clean,
      levels = c(
        "Control",
        "Probe = Imagined Color",
        "Probe \u2260 Imagined Color"
      )
    )
  )

summary_data <- accuracy_data %>%
  group_by(trial_type_clean) %>%
  summarise(
    mean_acc = mean(prop_correct, na.rm = TRUE),
    se = sd(prop_correct, na.rm = TRUE) / sqrt(n()),
    .groups = "drop"
  )

#### creates plot
accuracy_plot <- ggplot(
  accuracy_data, 
  aes(
    x = trial_type_clean, 
    y = prop_correct,
    ####uses tooltip to display participant id when hovering over data
    text = paste("Participant ID:", participant, "<br>Accuracy:", scales::percent(prop_correct, accuracy = 0.1))
  )
) +
  
  # Individual Lines
  geom_line(
    aes(group = participant),
    color = "gray70",
    alpha = 0.45,
    linewidth = 0.7
  ) +
  
  # Individual Points
  geom_point(
    aes(group = participant),
    color = "gray70",
    alpha = 0.5,
    size = 2.2
  ) +
  
  #### add gray line to indicate at-chance level (50%)
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0.5,
    linetype = "dashed",
    color = "gray40",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # --- Summary Layers (Must override text = NULL to avoid errors) ---
  
  # Summary Mean Line
  geom_line(
    data = summary_data,
    aes(y = mean_acc, group = 1, text = NULL), 
    color = "black",
    linewidth = 1.5
  ) +
  
  # Summary Error Bars
  geom_errorbar(
    data = summary_data,
    aes(
      y = mean_acc,
      ymin = mean_acc - se,
      ymax = mean_acc + se,
      text = NULL
    ),
    width = 0.12,
    linewidth = 1.1,
    color = "black"
  ) +
  
  # Summary Mean Points
  geom_point(
    data = summary_data,
    aes(y = mean_acc, text = NULL),
    size = 4
  ) +
  
  # --- Rest of your original design and scales ---
  scale_x_discrete(
    labels = function(x) str_wrap(x, width = 12),
    expand = expansion(mult = 0.2)
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    breaks = c(0, 0.5, 1),
    labels = c("0%", "50%", "100%"),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.02, 0.06))
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Objective Measure: Probe Detection",
    x = NULL,
    y = "Percent Correct"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal(base_size = 18) +
  theme(
    panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
    panel.grid.major.x = element_blank(),
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 16, angle = 0, hjust = 0.5, lineheight = 0.9),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 15),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 26)
  )

#makes plot interactive
ggplotly(accuracy_plot, tooltip = "text")
```

## Row
```{r }
#make dataframe with accuracy for each subj
subject_wide <- accuracy_data %>%
#round percentages for readability
  mutate(prop_correct = round(prop_correct, 3)) %>%
#separate trial types into columns
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = trial_type_clean,
    values_from = prop_correct
  ) %>%
  mutate(participant = as.character(participant))

#calc average for across subjects
across_subjects_row <- summary_data %>%
  mutate(mean_acc = round(mean_acc, 3)) %>%
  select(trial_type_clean, mean_acc) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = trial_type_clean,
    values_from = mean_acc
  ) %>%
##label row for aggregated data
  mutate(participant = "Across Subjects Summary")

###combine subj and aggregated data
final_table_data <- bind_rows(subject_wide, across_subjects_row)

#create table using gt pkg
final_table_data %>%
  gt(rowname_col = "participant") %>%
  
#formate percents
  fmt_percent(
    columns = everything(),
    decimals = 2
  ) %>%
  
#style to differentiate aggregated data
  tab_style(
    style = list(
      cell_fill(color = "#f7f7f7"),
      cell_text(weight = "bold")
    ),
    locations = cells_body(
      rows = (participant == "Across Subjects Summary")
    )
  ) %>%
  tab_style(
    style = cell_text(weight = "bold"),
    locations = cells_stub(
      rows = (participant == "Across Subjects Summary")
    )
  ) %>%
  
#border customization
  tab_options(
    table.border.top.color = "black",
    table.border.bottom.color = "black",
    column_labels.border.bottom.color = "black",
    column_labels.font.weight = "bold",
    stub.border.color = "gray80"
  ) %>%
  
  # Context titles
  tab_header(
    title = md("Objective Measure: Probe Accuracy"),

  )
```
